Operation 'getStatusSource' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. The current summary and description are identical and overly vague. They lack clarity about what 'source' means in this context (raw text vs. formatted content), what data is returned, authentication requirements, and ownership constraints. The improved version clarifies that this retrieves unformatted source content, explains the use case (editing), specifies authentication and ownership requirements, and indicates the response schema type for better AI agent understanding.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve status source content for editing
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the original source content of a status, including the raw text and any media descriptions. This endpoint is useful for editing existing statuses, as it provides the unformatted source material. Requires authentication and the status must belong to the authenticated user. Returns a StatusSource object containing the original text and media attachment descriptions.
data.reasoning: The current summary and description are identical and overly vague. They lack clarity about what 'source' means in this context (raw text vs. formatted content), what data is returned, authentication requirements, and ownership constraints. The improved version clarifies that this retrieves unformatted source content, explains the use case (editing), specifies authentication and ownership requirements, and indicates the response schema type for better AI agent understanding.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve accounts that boosted a status
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of accounts that have boosted (reblogged) the specified status. Requires the status ID as a path parameter. Returns an array of Account objects representing users who boosted the status. Useful for understanding the reach and engagement of a status.
data.reasoning: Original description is identical to summary and lacks essential details. Enhanced version specifies: the paginated nature of results, what parameter is required, what is returned (Account objects), and provides context about use cases for AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Adds the specified status to the authenticated user's favourites. Requires the status ID as a path parameter and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Returns the updated Status object with the favourited flag set to true. This action is reversible via the unfavouriteStatus endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks details about authentication requirements, return value, and reversibility. Enhanced version specifies authentication, return type, and side effects for better AI agent interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Removes the specified status from the authenticated user's favourites. Requires the status ID as a path parameter and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Returns the updated Status object with the favourited flag set to false. This reverses a previous favouriteStatus action.

data.description_suggestion: Boosts (reblogs) the specified status, sharing it with the authenticated user's followers. Requires the status ID as a path parameter and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Returns the updated Status object with the reblogged flag set to true. This action is reversible via the unboostStatus endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks context about what boosting does, authentication requirements, return value, and reversibility. Enhanced version explains the effect (sharing with followers), authentication, return type, and side effects for AI agent clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Adds the specified status to the authenticated user's bookmarks. Bookmarked statuses are saved for later reference and appear in the user's bookmarks collection. Returns the updated status object with bookmark state reflected. This action is idempotent - bookmarking an already bookmarked status has no additional effect.
data.reasoning: The current description is identical to the summary and lacks essential details. message: Operation 'unbookmarkStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too brief and lacks operational context. data.description_suggestion: Removes the specified status from the authenticated user's bookmarks collection. The status itself remains unaffected; only the bookmark association is removed. Returns the updated status object with bookmark state reflected. This action is idempotent - unbookmarking a non-bookmarked status has no effect.

message: Operation 'muteStatusConversation' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks clarity about what muting actually does (suppresses notifications, not visibility). data.description_suggestion: Mutes notifications for the conversation thread containing the specified status. When muted, the authenticated user will not receive notifications for replies or interactions within this thread, though the thread remains visible in their timeline. Returns the updated status object. Muting affects only the authenticated user's notification preferences.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks clarity about what muting actually does (suppresses notifications, not visibility). Added context about notification behavior, visibility, scope (authenticated user only), and return value to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's effects.

data.description_suggestion: Restores notifications for the conversation thread containing the specified status. After unmuting, the authenticated user will receive notifications for replies and interactions within this thread again. Returns the updated status object. This reverses a previous mute action.

data.description_suggestion: Pins the specified status to the authenticated user's profile, making it prominently displayed at the top of their profile page. Only the status author can pin their own statuses. Returns the updated status object with pin state reflected. Users can typically pin a limited number of statuses (server-dependent).

Returns a StatusSource object containing the original text and media attachment descriptions.
